message.content:
You are an adversarial refuter. ASSUME this finding is FALSE and try to prove it. Read the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and find the code path / contract / test that makes the PoC impossible or the concern moot. Only conclude survives=true if you genuinely cannot disprove it.

Finding O-2: run_study never persists the provenance trail or attaches it to the MLflow run — auto-path provenance is volatile and uncorrelated
File: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:351
Description: run_study records a rich hash-chained provenance trail and stores the experiment_run_id INSIDE the trail's run_end entry (line 717-723), but it never (a) writes the trail anywhere durable, nor (b) attaches the trail to the experiment run via tracker.log_artifact, even when a tracker is present and run_id is known. The trail lives only in StudyResult.provenance (in-memory). Persistence is entirely opt-in: a caller must remember to call StudyResult.persist_trail() and then tracker.log_artifact() (documented at pipeline.py:226-244 and tracking.py:92). Consequently the default governed run produces an MLflow run with metrics but NO link back to the append-only audit trail, and the trail evaporates on process exit. GOVERNANCE §3 calls the trail 'the spine of reproducibility and of the independence claim', yet the composed runner — the engine-owned guarantee — does not durably emit it. The correlation is also one-directional: the trail names the run_id, but the run has no artifact pointing back to the trail.
PoC: Call run_study(plan, gate_handler=auto_approve, tracker=MlflowExperimentTracker()). On success you get a StudyResult; the MLflow run has params/metrics but zero artifacts. Inspect the MLflow run: there is no provenance.json. Let the process exit: StudyResult.provenance is gone. An auditor opening the MLflow run cannot retrieve or verify the hash-chained trail that produced the recorded metrics.

Return survives=false if you can refute it (with the specific reason), survives=true if it withstands a genuine disproof attempt.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

211	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
212	class StudyResult:
213	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
214	
215	    study: str
216	    corpus_fingerprint: str
217	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
218	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
219	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None

226	    def persist_trail(self, path: str | Path) -> Path:
227	        """Write the hash-chained provenance trail to ``path`` as JSON and return it (#F-045).
228	
229	        ``run_study`` keeps the trail only in memory (``self.provenance``); a caller that needs a
230	        durable, re-verifiable audit trail across process exit must persist it. The written
231	        records round-trip through :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.from_records` and re-check via
232	        :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.verify_records`. Call ``tracker.log_artifact`` on the
233	        returned path to attach it to the experiment run.
234	        """
235	        import json
236	
237	        from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
238	
239	        out = Path(path)
240	        out.write_text(
241	            json.dumps(list(entries_to_records(list(self.provenance))), indent=2),
242	            encoding="utf-8",
243	        )
244	        return out
245	
246	
247	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
248	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
249	
250	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
251	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
252	
253	    return _log

256	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
257	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
258	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
259	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
260	
261	
262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343	
344	    trail.record(
345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
357	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
358	                },
359	            )
360	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
361	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
362	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
363	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
364	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
365	            _log.warning(
366	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
367	                "to the tracker for this study",
368	                type(exc).__name__,
369	                exc,
370	            )
371	
372	    ok = False
373	    started_at = time.monotonic()
374	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
375	    try:
376	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
377	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
378	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
379	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
380	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
381	        bootstrap_tracing()
382	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
383	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
384	        # ---- INGEST ----
385	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
386	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
387	            try:
388	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
389	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
390	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
391	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
392	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
393	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
394	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
395	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
396	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
397	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
398	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
399	                raise IngestionError(

650	                        label=v.label.value,
651	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
652	                    )
653	
654	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
655	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
656	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
657	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
658	                    baseline,
659	                    claim_records,
660	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
661	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
662	                    authority=plan.authority,
663	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
664	                    source_name=defn.name,
665	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
666	                )
667	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
668	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
669	                trail.record(
670	                    "score",
671	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
672	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
673	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
674	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
675	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
676	                )
677	                sc = scorecard
678	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
679	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
680	                track(
681	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
682	                )
683	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
684	                if ar is not None:
685	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
686	
687	        # ---- REPORT ----
688	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
689	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
690	            trail.record(
691	                "report",
692	                "assembled reproducibility package",
693	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
694	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
695	            )
696	
697	        if tuple(visited) != required:
698	            raise FirewallViolation(
699	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
700	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
701	            )
702	        if verify_trail:
703	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
704	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
705	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
706	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
707	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
708	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
709	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
710	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
711	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
712	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
713	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
714	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
715	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
716	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
717	        trail.record(
718	            "run_end",
719	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
720	            study=defn.name,
721	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
722	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
723	        )
724	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
725	        ok = True
726	    except Exception as exc:
727	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
728	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
729	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
730	        try:
731	            trail.record(
732	                "run_failed",
733	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
734	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
735	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
736	                last_phase=last_phase,
737	            )
738	        except Exception as inner:  # noqa: BLE001 — never mask the original failure
739	            # The run_failed entry could not be recorded (payload unserializable, clock raised,
740	            # trail lock contended). Don't silently swallow — emit a structured warning so the
741	            # failure reason is at least in the logs, even though it is absent from the trail
742	            # (pass 3, #F-030). The original exception is still re-raised below.
743	            _log.warning(
744	                "could not record run_failed provenance entry (%s: %s)",
745	                type(inner).__name__,
746	                inner,
747	            )
748	        raise
749	    finally:
750	        trace_ctx.close()
751	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
752	            try:
753	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
754	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
755	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
756	                pass
757	
758	    return StudyResult(
759	        study=defn.name,
760	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
761	        baseline=baseline,
762	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
763	        scorecard=scorecard,
764	        phases=tuple(visited),
765	        provenance=trail.entries,
766	        source_version=source_version,
767	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
768	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
769	    )

content.text: The finding claims `run_study` never persists the trail nor attaches it to the MLflow run. Let me search the code thoroughly for any `log_artifact` call or trail persistence within the run flow, in case I'm missing something.

content.text: The only references to `log_artifact` are: the protocol definition (tracking.py:37), the MLflow impl (tracking.py:92), and documentation in `persist_trail`'s docstring. There is NO `log_artifact` call inside `run_study`. Let me confirm there's no wrapper/runner that does this, and check the full `run_study` body for any artifact emission I missed.

content.text: Confirmed: no production code path calls `log_artifact` to attach the trail, and no runner wraps `run_study` to persist it. The only callers are tests and an example. Let me verify the example and the test at line 436 to see if there's any engine-owned persistence, and check the GOVERNANCE doc claim.

461	def test_versioner_failure_is_wrapped_as_ingestion_error(tmp_path):
462	    # #F-008: a storage failure in the optional versioner seam must surface as the documented
463	    # IngestionError, not a raw untyped exception the caller cannot distinguish from a firewall
464	    # breach. Pre-fix: versioner.put() was called bare and PermissionError propagated raw.
465	    from assay_engine.pipeline import IngestionError
466	
467	    class BoomVersioner:
468	        def put(self, ref_str):
469	            raise PermissionError("read-only store")
470	
471	        def path_for(self, version):  # pragma: no cover - not reached
472	            raise AssertionError
473	
474	    with pytest.raises(IngestionError, match="data versioner failed"):
475	        _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY, versioner=BoomVersioner())

478	def test_confirm_held_out_returning_none_raises_firewall_violation(tmp_path):
479	    # #F-021: a confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; the runner must raise a typed
480	    # FirewallViolation, not die on an opaque AttributeError at verdict.hypothesis_id.
481	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
482	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="expected a Verdict"):
483	        run_study(replace(plan, confirm_held_out=lambda h, c: None), gate_handler=auto_approve)

487	    # #F-042: a discover() returning no hypotheses is a vacuous run; the pipeline must fail loud
488	    # rather than complete with zero verdicts (indistinguishable from a broken callable).
489	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
490	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="no corpus units|no hypotheses|discover"):
491	        run_study(replace(plan, discover=lambda c: []), gate_handler=auto_approve)

494	def test_tracker_start_run_failure_warns_and_continues(tmp_path, caplog):
495	    # #F-028: if start_run fails, run_id stays None and ALL metrics silently no-op. The engine
496	    # must emit a structured warning (and a trail entry) so an operator seeing no MLflow run
497	    # knows why, instead of guessing. The run itself still completes.
498	    import logging
499	
500	    class StartBoomTracker:
501	        def start_run(self, name, params):
502	            raise RuntimeError("tracker unreachable")
503	
504	        def log_metric(self, *a):  # pragma: no cover - never reached (run_id is None)
505	            raise AssertionError
506	
507	        def log_artifact(self, *a):  # pragma: no cover
508	            raise AssertionError
509	
510	        def end_run(self, *a, **k):  # pragma: no cover - skipped when run_id is None
511	            raise AssertionError
512	
513	    with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger="assay_engine.pipeline"):
514	        res = _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY, tracker=StartBoomTracker())

content.text: The test at line 436 defines a `log_artifact` on the fake tracker, but the assertions at 444-450 never assert any `("artifact", ...)` call — confirming the engine does not emit one. Let me read the GOVERNANCE §3 context around line 89-91 to confirm the doc claim and check whether ADR-0010 explicitly scopes persistence as caller's responsibility (which would be the strongest refutation candidate).

content.content:
80	> and take an optional `authority=` to opt into the full check; a study that confirms outside a
81	> runner must pass it (or call `require_preregistered` itself). `Hypothesis.locked` is only a
82	> presence predicate; `methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered` is the
83	> methodology-grade check.
84	
85	## 3. Provenance
86	
87	An **append-only** audit trail (W3C PROV-DM style) records every action before the next
88	executes. No step can be retroactively removed or reordered. This is the spine of
89	reproducibility and of the independence claim.
90	
91	> **Implementation status (engine):** wired (ADR-0010). `assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`
92	> is an append-only, hash-chained trail; the study runner (`assay_engine.pipeline.run_study`)
93	> records every action — ingest, blind baseline, each locked hypothesis (discovery *and*
94	> claim-derived), every gate decision, each verdict, the scorecard, the report — *before the next
95	> runs*, and verifies the chain before returning. There is no remove/edit/reorder API.
96	> **Integrity, honestly, in two tiers:** the default unkeyed chain is *tamper-evident against
97	> naive tampering and accidental corruption* (a single-entry edit, a reorder, or a deletion is
98	> caught by `verify_records`) but is **not** forgery-proof — a party who controls the serialized
99	> bytes can edit a payload and recompute the whole SHA-256 chain. Pass a `secret=` and the chain
100	> is HMAC-keyed, so the head cannot be recomputed without the secret (a downstream store cannot
101	> silently rewrite and re-seal history); `verify_records` must be given the same secret. An
102	> in-memory trail is still as trustworthy as its process, and non-repudiable third-party
103	> attestation of the trail's *time* is the same pluggable concern as pre-registration (§2),
104	> out of scope here. A caller may pass its own trail to `run_study` so a run that *raises* (a
105	> blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail.
106	>
107	> The synchronous `run_study` path records all of the above into the chained trail by default.
108	> The **durable LangGraph gate path** (`make_gate_node`) records decisions into LangGraph
109	> `gate_decisions` *state*, which is not the hash-chained trail; to bring that path under §3
110	> integrity, wire `make_gate_node(..., recorder=...)` to a `ProvenanceTrail` (the recorder
111	> bridge, #111). Without that bridge, the graph path's gate decisions are graph state, not
112	> tamper-evident trail entries.
113	
114	## 4. Single-operator structural independence
115	
116	Independence from operator bias is achieved structurally, not by a co-reviewer:
117	
118	- **Code gates** the operator cannot silently bypass.
119	- **Append-only provenance** that records the full decision history.
120	- **Timestamped pre-registration** committing to hypotheses before they are tested.
121	- **A complete reproducibility package** published with findings.
122	
123	This is a deliberate methodological position and is stated explicitly in any output.
124

40	   records provenance, and enforces the methodology by construction —
41	   - BASELINE is built inside a sealed `ClaimBlindGuard` (Firewall A) and its fingerprint must
42	     match the ingested corpus;
43	   - DISCOVERY hands the discover step only the discovery partition; PREREGISTER verifies every
44	     hypothesis with `require_preregistered`; a **governance gate** reviews the locked hypotheses
45	     before CONFIRM, which tests only the held-out partition (Firewall B);
46	   - ADJUDICATE reuses the *single* blind baseline via `adjudicate_with_baseline` (factored out of
47	     `adjudicate` for this) with `not_after` = the instant captured *before* the baseline build, so
48	     the lock-before-baseline guarantee survives the shared-baseline composition;
49	   - the visited phase sequence must equal the mode's required sequence (no skip/reorder/extra),
50	     and the provenance chain must verify, before a `StudyResult` is returned.
51	
52	   A study supplies the *domain* via a `StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
53	   discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, claims source); the engine owns the *order, the
54	   firewalls, the gate, the provenance, and the tracing*. The runner imports no adapter.
55	
56	3. **Mode-accurate phases.** Building the composition exposed that `required_phases` forced the
57	   discovery spine (DISCOVERY/PREREGISTER/CONFIRM) onto *every* study, including adjudicate-only
58	   ones — which derive hypotheses from claims, not data. The modes are independent, so the spine
59	   is now conditional on `DISCOVERY` mode, mirroring how ADJUDICATE/SCORE are conditional on
60	   adjudication mode. An adjudicate-only study is INGEST→BASELINE→ADJUDICATE→SCORE→REPORT.
61	
62	4. **The governance gate is a pluggable handoff, before *every* confirmatory step.** `run_study`
63	   takes a `gate_handler` (`GateReview → GateDecision`); the default auto-approves and records, and
64	   an operator/LangGraph handler can block (raising `GateError`) or park. There are two gates so
65	   no confirmatory step is ungated (adversarial review #86): `review-locked-hypotheses` before
66	   CONFIRM (discovery), and `review-baseline-and-claims` before ADJUDICATE (adjudication). The
67	   decision's `approved` is snapshotted once and used for both the recorded entry and the
68	   control-flow branch, so it cannot diverge (#94). A caller may pass its own `trail` to
69	   `run_study` so a *blocked* run still leaves an auditable partial trail including the blocking
70	   decision (#92).
71	
72	## Consequences
73	
74	- There is now a single, engine-owned, **runnable** end-to-end workflow, verified both purely
75	  (`tests/test_pipeline.py`, both modes + negative paths) and **live** against a real backend
76	  (`tests/integration/test_pipeline_live.py` — a real OpenTelemetry span fires around every phase
77	  handoff, with an intact provenance chain). The reference adapter (`tests/reference_study.py`) is
78	  the dataset-agnostic demonstration of the workflow.
79	- GOVERNANCE §3 is honoured by construction, not promised. Honest scope: an in-memory trail is as
80	  trustworthy as its process; the hash chain's value is that a *serialized* trail round-trips only
81	  if untouched, so an external store/reviewer can verify integrity. Non-repudiable third-party
82	  attestation of the trail's *time* is the same pluggable concern as pre-registration (ADR-0009),
83	  out of scope here.
84	- The lock-before-baseline ordering for adjudication is preserved across the shared-baseline

content.text:
This is decisive. The finding is substantively confirmed, and the docs corroborate rather than refute it. Key evidence:

1. **No production code path persists or attaches the trail.** Grep across all of `src/` finds `log_artifact` only in the protocol/impl (tracking.py) and in `persist_trail`'s *docstring*. `run_study` never calls `persist_trail` or `tracker.log_artifact`. The trail lives only in `StudyResult.provenance` (in-memory), set at pipeline.py:765.

2. **The run_end entry stores `experiment_run_id=run_id` (pipeline.py:721)** but nothing writes a reverse pointer (artifact) from the MLflow run back to the trail — exactly the one-directional correlation the finding describes.

3. **The docs explicitly scope persistence to the caller.** ADR-0010:79-83 and GOVERNANCE §3 both concede "an in-memory trail is as trustworthy as its process" and that the hash chain's value materializes only for a *serialized* trail an external store/reviewer can verify. `persist_trail`'s docstring (pipeline.py:229-233) states the caller "must" persist and "Call `tracker.log_artifact` on the returned path to attach it." So persistence/attachment is by design opt-in — confirming the finding's mechanism, not refuting it.

4. **The test corroborates the gap.** `tests/test_pipeline.py:436` even gives the fake tracker a `log_artifact` method, yet the assertions (444-450) check only start/metric/end calls — never an `("artifact", ...)` entry — because the engine never emits one.

My only attempt at refutation would be to argue this is "intended scope, not a bug." But the finding is framed as a factual/contract claim (the engine-owned composed runner does not durably emit or back-link the trail under a default governed run), and that claim is true. The docs calling persistence the caller's job is *acknowledgement* of the gap, not a contract that makes the PoC impossible. The PoC — `run_study(plan, gate_handler=auto_approve, tracker=MlflowExperimentTracker())` yields an MLflow run with metrics but zero artifacts, and an in-memory-only trail — executes exactly as described.

I cannot disprove it.

input.reasoning: Confirmed by direct code inspection; could not disprove. (a) No production path persists or attaches the trail: grepping all of src/ shows `log_artifact` only in the protocol (observability/tracking.py:37) and its MLflow impl (tracking.py:92), plus the persist_trail docstring (pipeline.py:232). run_study (pipeline.py:262-769) never calls persist_trail or tracker.log_artifact. (b) The trail is handed back only in-memory via StudyResult.provenance=trail.entries (pipeline.py:765); StudyResult.persist_trail (pipeline.py:226-244) is opt-in and its docstring explicitly tells the caller they 'must' persist and then 'Call tracker.log_artifact on the returned path to attach it.' (c) The run_end entry stores experiment_run_id=run_id (pipeline.py:721) but nothing writes a reverse artifact pointer from the MLflow run to the trail — matching the one-directional correlation in the finding. (d) The docs corroborate rather than refute: GOVERNANCE §3 (docs/GOVERNANCE.md:88-89) calls the trail 'the spine of reproducibility and of the independence claim,' while ADR-0010:79-83 and GOVERNANCE §3 concede the trail is 'as trustworthy as its process' and that the hash chain's value only materializes for a serialized trail an external reviewer can verify — i.e., persistence is acknowledged as caller-scoped, not engine-guaranteed. (e) Test corroboration: tests/test_pipeline.py:436 gives the fake tracker a log_artifact method, yet assertions at 444-450 verify only start/metric/end calls and never assert an ('artifact', ...) call, because the engine emits none. The PoC executes exactly as described: a default governed run with an MlflowExperimentTracker yields a run with params/metrics but zero artifacts, and the trail evaporates on process exit. The only counter-argument is 'intended scope,' but that is an acknowledgement of the gap, not a code path/contract/test that makes the PoC impossible.

content.text: Finding O-2 survives. I confirmed through direct inspection that `run_study` (`/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py`) never persists the provenance trail nor attaches it to the MLflow run, the only `log_artifact` references in `src/` are the protocol/impl and a docstring, and the docs (ADR-0010, GOVERNANCE §3) acknowledge rather than refute the gap.
